The model favors Mexico at 71%, with Czech Republic capturing only 7% of win probability. Mexico enters as the clear statistical favorite under the neutral venue conditions.

The spread reflects a substantially lopsided matchup. Mexico's draw probability sits at 23%, suggesting the model sees meaningful risk of a stalemate but expects decisive play to favor the favorite. Czech Republic's 7% win chance indicates the model rates an upset as unlikely, though not impossible.

No sportsbook consensus is available yet for this fixture. Once market odds emerge, comparison with the model's 71% Mexico win probability will clarify whether bookmakers align with the statistical baseline or price the teams differently.
